entities.name: KABULI CHICKPEA
entities.description: A type of chickpea (Cicer arietinum L.) used in the study to investigate seed size inheritance.

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The phenotypic characteristic of the seed dimensions being studied for inheritance.

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The quantitative measurement used to evaluate seed size, specifically the weight of 100 seeds.

entities.name: ICCV 2
entities.description: A small-seeded kabuli chickpea cultivar used as one of the parents in the genetic cross.

entities.name: L 550
entities.description: A small-seeded kabuli chickpea cultivar used as one of the parents in the genetic cross.

entities.name: F1 GENERATION
entities.description: The first filial generation resulting from the cross between ICCV 2 and L 550.

entities.name: F2 GENERATION
entities.description: The second filial generation used to observe segregation patterns and transgressive segregants.

entities.name: BACKCROSS GENERATION
entities.description: The offspring resulting from crossing the F1 generation back to the parental lines.

entities.name: TRANSGRESSIVE SEGREGATION
entities.description: The appearance of offspring in the F2 generation that exceed the parental range for seed size.

entities.name: ADDITIVE EFFECTS
entities.description: The genetic interaction where the effect of alleles at different loci adds up to determine the phenotype.

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: The genetic loci responsible for controlling the seed size trait in kabuli chickpea.

entities.name: RATIO ANALYSIS
entities.description: The statistical method of comparing observed segregation ratios to expected ratios using chi-square tests to determine genetic control.
